summary: v2 redesign verified end-to-end. Backend: 18/18 pytest tests pass (100%) including new MAX_PLANTS=6 enforcement and image-field assertions. Frontend: 3x2 slot grid renders, 6-plant catalog dialog with images, AI floating button + slide-out drawer (Claude Sonnet model selector + Tutorials toggle + chat input working with real LLM response), critical water/nutrient alerts trigger on low levels, control panel sliders functional, notification bell badge visible, community tab loads.

issues: Alert wording differs between My Tent ('Water level is low — refill needed') and Control Panel ('Water level is low! Refill soon.') - minor copy inconsistency, not a bug
Console warning: Missing aria-describedby for DialogContent (Radix dialog accessibility) - non-blocking

critical_code_review_comments: check_notifications still inserts duplicate low_water/low_nutrients notifications on every call (no dedup like harvest_ready) - persists from v1, observed badge count growing on repeated checks.
Strawberry pexels image URL was flagged in request as potentially flaky; loaded fine during this run but worth monitoring.
server.py is 462 lines - within limit. MongoDB _id properly excluded everywhere. Env vars used correctly.
Dialog components missing DialogDescription / aria-describedby - accessibility nit (Radix warning in console).
seed_data_creation: TEST_-prefixed plants created and cleaned up in test_max_plants_limit. test_create_plant left a TEST_Lettuce that was deleted by test_delete_plant. Spinach was added via UI test (not cleaned up - 4 plants now growing: Lettuce, Basil, Mint, Spinach).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py cover the new v2 requirements: 6-plant catalog with images, MAX_PLANTS=6 (HTTP 400), image field in POST /api/plants response. There are now 4 growing plants in DB (Lettuce, Basil, Mint, Spinach) after this iteration's UI test. AI chat verified with real Claude response (model selector defaults to 'Claude Sonnet'). Tent status was restored to defaults after low-water alert test.
rca of the issue: No bugs; redesign requirements all met.
